tldr: Congressional trading over the recent period is heavily dominated by mega-cap tech and semiconductor accumulation, with significant cluster buying in MSFT, NVDA, AAPL, and AMZN. However, the most notable individual actions include a massive $5M+ purchase of AESI by Chip Roy and high-profile buys of INTC and UBER by Nancy Pelosi. Meanwhile, some members are taking profits in high-flying tech and medical device names, evidenced by large sells in QCOM, NVDA, PODD, and DXCM.

highlights.headline: Chip Roy's Massive AESI Purchase
highlights.detail: Rep. Chip Roy executed a massive $5.0M–$25.0M purchase of Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) in late April, making it the largest single directional trade in the dataset, though he did trim a small $100K-$250K fraction a couple of weeks later.

highlights.headline: Pelosi Buys INTC and UBER
highlights.detail: Rep. Nancy Pelosi disclosed a $1.0M–$5.0M purchase of Intel (INTC) and a $500K–$1.0M purchase of Uber (UBER) in late May, signaling continued conviction in legacy semiconductors and gig-economy tech.

highlights.headline: Broad Consensus in AI and Mega-Cap Tech
highlights.detail: Members are heavily clustering into AI and mega-cap tech, with MSFT (30 members), AAPL (27 members), NVDA (17 members), and AMZN (16 members) seeing the highest breadth of buying activity.

highlights.headline: Trimming QCOM and NVDA
highlights.detail: Despite the broader cluster buying in semis, some members are taking profits. Sara Jacobs sold between $1.0M and $2.0M of Qualcomm (QCOM) across two trades, while Sheldon Whitehouse sold up to $500K of Nvidia (NVDA).

highlights.headline: Tina Smith Exits Medical Devices
highlights.detail: Sen. Tina Smith's spouse executed a series of sales in the medical device sector, offloading up to $500K each in Insulet (PODD) and DexCom (DXCM) across late April and early May.

highlights.headline: Gottheimer's Simultaneous MSFT Trading
highlights.detail: Rep. Josh Gottheimer executed simultaneous buys and sells of Microsoft (MSFT) in the $750K-$1.5M range on the same day, likely indicating options exercises or routine portfolio rebalancing rather than new directional conviction.
